meta_title: For Students: marketing automation to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Marketing Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building a marketing automation tool. A step-by-step beginner guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' trap, build a marketing automation tool that solves a real-world pain point. By automating lead generation or content scheduling using Antigravity and Claude Code, you demonstrate technical competency, architectural awareness, and the ability to ship production-grade software that recruiters actually value over generic tutorials.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of junior careers. You have the theory, you have the passion, but you lack the tangible proof that you can ship a solution that works in the wild. Employers don't care about your To-Do list app; they care about your ability to automate complexity.

We are going to bridge that gap. By building a functional marketing automation engine, you aren't just writing code—you are solving a business problem. This is your chance to transform from a student into an AI-driven engineer who understands how to leverage modern developer tools to produce high-impact software.

steps.title: Define the Automation Logic
steps.description: Identify a repetitive task like 'social media sentiment monitoring' or 'automated email drafting'. Clarify the input source and the desired output action.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Draft a technical specification for a marketing automation tool that pulls data from a CSV, processes it via an LLM, and outputs a formatted draft.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project folder structure for a marketing automation tool with a README, src/ folder, and requirements.txt'

steps.title: Integrate Data Sources
steps.description: Connect your app to a live data source. Focus on clean, readable APIs that provide real-world data, proving you can handle external dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Python module using requests to fetch recent posts from a public API, ensuring robust error handling for rate limits and connection timeouts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement an API client in src/api.py that fetches data and handles common HTTP exceptions'

steps.title: Build the Logic Engine
steps.description: Implement the AI logic that processes the data. This is the core of your automation, demonstrating your ability to prompt-engineer effectively within an application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer that sends the API response to an LLM with a system prompt optimized for creating professional marketing copy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a service in src/engine.py that processes strings and returns structured JSON responses'

steps.title: Implement Execution Hooks
steps.description: Create the final step of the automation loop that triggers the output, such as saving to a database or posting to a webhook.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a storage utility that saves the AI-generated marketing content into a local SQLite database for persistence and auditing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a database utility in src/db.py and integrate it into the main execution flow'

steps.title: Refactor and Document
steps.description: Clean up your code and ensure it is production-ready. Good code is readable code; documentation is what gets you hired.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the entire codebase for PEP8 compliance and add docstrings to every function. Suggest improvements for modularity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the code for better modularity and generate a comprehensive documentation file'

pro_tips: Always wrap external API calls in a retry logic decorator to handle transient network failures gracefully.
Use environment variables for all API keys; hardcoding secrets is an immediate red flag for hiring managers.
Include a 'dry-run' mode in your CLI so users can test the automation without triggering real external actions.
Create a 'demo.mp4' screen recording of your tool running to embed directly in your GitHub README.
Focus on error logging; a tool that tells you exactly why it failed is infinitely more valuable than one that silently crashes.
content.next_steps: Deploy your tool to a cloud provider like Vercel or Render, and share the GitHub repository link on your LinkedIn profile with a summary of the problem you solved.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the technical foundation to build real-world tools that move the needle. Don't let your code sit in a local folder—ship it, show it off, and start building your reputation as a high-impact developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
